name: Grilled Trout with Parsley-Caper Sauce
description: Whole rainbow trout stuffed with lemon and thyme, grilled until the skin crisps, finished with a bright parsley-caper sauce.
recipeIngredient: 2 whole rainbow trout (10-12 oz each), cleaned
recipeInstructions: Pat the trout dry, season inside and out, and stuff the cavities with lemon rings and thyme sprigs. Rub the skin with oil.
Heat a grill to very hot (~500F). Grill the trout until the skin is crisp and the flesh flakes, ~5 minutes per side.
Whisk the sauce ingredients together. Spoon over the grilled trout to serve.
author: The Mediterranean Dish

ai.cliffNotes: Clean grates, an aromatic-stuffed cavity, and tools-at-the-ready are what make whole grilled trout work.
keyTips: Clean the grill well before and after heating — delicate fish skin sticks otherwise.
Stuff the cavity fully with lemon and thyme to keep the flesh moist and flavor it from the inside.
Lay out all filleting tools beforehand so you can work fast once the hot fish comes off.
